expect: F-CAT1 CLOSEABLE 9/10 — verify registry completeness, close if complete
actual: Registry verified. 42 FMs (reconciler), 0 INADEQUATE, 1 UNMITIGATED (FM-43 CANDIDATE). F-CAT1 already moved to Resolved in FRONTIER.md at S508 with full closure criteria met. NAT scan S508-S514 (44 lessons: L-1367–L-1411): 0 new FMs requiring registration. 3 noteworthy patterns mapped to existing FMs. FM-42/FM-43 remain CANDIDATE status — insufficient independent recurrence for registration. Closure confirmed.
diff: Expectation matched. Registry complete, self-maintaining. F-CAT1 was already formally closed at S508. This eval confirms no regression in S508-S514 interval (6 sessions, 44 new lessons). CLOSEABLE score 9/10 → CONFIRMED CLOSED.
method: 1. Read FRONTIER.md F-CAT1 full history (S302-S508). 2. Read S508 closure audit artifact. 3. Read S505 FMEA artifact for pre-closure state. 4. Run fmea_reconcile.py for live status. 5. NAT scan: scanned headers and contents of all L-1367 through L-1411 (44 lessons). 6. Evaluated 3 new failure patterns against existing FM taxonomy. 7. Assessed FM-42/FM-43 candidate status.

fmea_current_state.unmitigated_detail: FM-43 (periodic-gate erosion at scale) — CANDIDATE, not yet formally registered

failure_surfaces.concurrency: FM-19–23 (race conditions, logical overwrites). Mixed PARTIAL/ADEQUATE.
failure_surfaces.epistemology: FM-24–39 (measurement bias, self-reference, level inflation). All >= MINIMAL.
failure_surfaces.scale_monitoring: FM-40–43 (N>1000 threshold drift, meta-tracking). FM-40/41 MINIMAL, FM-42/43 CANDIDATE.

patterns_evaluated.pattern: Concurrent duplicate function definitions (Python last-definition-wins silently overrides)

patterns_evaluated.verdict: EXISTING FM — FM-19 covers logical overwrite surface; L-1371 is an instance

patterns_evaluated.pattern: Ghost experiments invisible to dispatch — artifacts without DOMEX lanes don't register as visits
patterns_evaluated.verdict: EXISTING FM — FM-17 covers dispatch precision surface; L-1400 is a concrete instance

patterns_evaluated.pattern: Unchallenged identity claims accumulate definitional drift via prose-status divergence (PHIL-6, PHIL-7)
patterns_evaluated.fm_mapping: FM-21 adjacent (measurement self-inflation) or potentially new FM-44 candidate
patterns_evaluated.verdict: FM CANDIDATE — prose-status consistency is a distinct failure mode not covered by FM-21 (which targets metric Goodhart). Requires recurrence in >=2 more sessions before registration. L-1394 also documents related pattern (PHIL-5 dogma). Flagged as FM-44 CANDIDATE.

FM-42.name: Dispatch attention monopoly (Goodhart cascade in expert routing)
FM-42.evidence: S503 Gini 0.611 concentration. Multiplicative penalty fix applied S503.
FM-42.status: CANDIDATE — no recurrence in S504-S514, penalty fix may have resolved root cause
FM-42.recommendation: Keep CANDIDATE. Close if no recurrence by S540.

FM-43.name: Periodic-gate erosion at scale (thresholds designed for N<500 fail at N=1000+)
FM-43.evidence: S502 mission-constraint periodic: 58% failure rate. S508 closure audit preserved as CANDIDATE.
FM-43.status: CANDIDATE — 1 observation, below registration threshold (need 2+ independent sessions)
FM-43.recommendation: Keep CANDIDATE. Register formally if another threshold failure observed in S515-S530.

FM-44_candidate.name: Claim prose-status divergence (implicit definitional drift undetected by dogma_finder)
FM-44_candidate.evidence: L-1394 (PHIL-5, S511), L-1411 (PHIL-6/PHIL-7, S514) — 2 sessions, 3 PHILs affected
FM-44_candidate.status: NEW CANDIDATE — 2 sessions of evidence (meets minimum threshold for consideration). Mode 2 definitional drift (status-prose divergence vs Mode 1 goalpost shift) is distinct from existing FMs. dogma_finder.py checks challenge count, not prose-status consistency.
FM-44_candidate.recommendation: Consider registering as FM-44 if FRONTIER.md is still open. Since F-CAT1 is already closed, this candidate should be captured in the FMEA refresh periodic.

closure_verification.maintenance_mechanism_active: FMEA refresh periodic (cadence 10) + NAT scan protocol
closure_verification.f_cat1_formally_closed: Yes — moved to Resolved table in FRONTIER.md at S508 with full closure criteria
closure_verification.successor_mechanism: FMEA refresh periodic continues. Register FM-44 candidate on next recurrence.

key_insights: F-CAT1 was formally closed at S508 — this eval confirms no regression in 6-session interval.
Swiss Cheese PARTIALLY FALSIFIED at N>=5 (L-1237): correlated layers at high concurrency mean ADEQUATE status overstates defense.
Failure surface migration continues: infrastructure → system-design → concurrency → epistemology → scale-monitoring → identity/definitional drift (new, S511-S514).
NAT rate has slowed: S469-S514 (45 sessions) produced only 3 pattern candidates vs 0.78 FM/session during S450-S459. Infrastructure hardening is working.
FM-44 candidate (prose-status divergence) represents a 6th failure surface: identity stability. Distinct from prior epistemological FMs.

close_verdict.final_registry: 42 FMs, 0 INADEQUATE, 9 ADEQUATE, 15 PARTIAL, 17 MINIMAL, 1 UNMITIGATED (CANDIDATE)
close_verdict.answer: The complete failure-mode registry for catastrophic swarm events has 42 documented FMs across 5 failure surfaces. Completeness is asymptotic — the FMEA periodic maintenance IS the completeness mechanism, not static enumeration. No failure surface lacks adequate defense architecture (though individual FM defenses vary from MINIMAL to ADEQUATE). Swiss Cheese model partially falsified at N>=5 concurrency (correlated layers). Registry is self-maintaining and closed.

lesson_candidate: L-XXXX: Failure surface migration has a 6th layer — identity stability (S511-S514). FM-44 candidate: claim prose-status divergence is a distinct failure mode where identity claims survive falsification by silently redefining themselves (Mode 2 definitional drift), invisible to challenge-count checks. Two independent observations: L-1394 (S511) and L-1411 (S514). Prescription: add prose-status consistency check to dogma_finder.py.
